name: nextjs16-skills
description: Key facts and links for Next.js 16. Use for planning, writing, and troubleshooting Next.js 16 changes.

name: webapp-testing
description: Project-level toolkit for testing NS Scrap ERP local web applications with Playwright. Use for legacy index.html checks, Vue/Vite route smoke tests, frontend behavior debugging, screenshots, browser logs, auth/permission UI flows, and UAT readiness.

name: supabase
description: Use when doing ANY task involving Supabase. Triggers: Supabase products (Database, Auth, Edge Functions, Realtime, Storage, Vectors, Cron, Queues); client libraries and SSR integrations (supabase-js, @supabase/ssr) in Next.js, React, SvelteKit, Astro, Remix; auth issues (login, logout, sessions, JWT, cookies, getSession, getUser, getClaims, RLS); Supabase CLI or MCP server; schema changes, migrations, security audits, Postgres extensions (pg_graphql, pg_cron, pg_vector).

name: supabase-postgres-best-practices
description: Postgres performance optimization and best practices from Supabase. Use this skill when writing, reviewing, or optimizing Postgres queries, schema designs, or database configurations.

name: ns-scrap-erp-quality-audit
description: Project-specific quality audit workflow for NS Scrap ERP. Use in this repo when reviewing legacy UI behavior, click paths, shared state side effects, browser smoke tests, accessibility, responsive layout, release readiness, workspace tooling, or final verification after a migration/refactor slice. Always read AGENTS.md first.

name: codebase-migrate
description: Run NS Scrap ERP large codebase migrations and multi-file refactors in local reviewable batches. Use for legacy-to-Vue route migration, schema cleanup, repeated transforms, auth/permission refactors, and module-by-module modernization. Do not assume Composio CLI, Linear, Jira, or GitHub PR automation unless explicitly requested.

name: ns-scrap-erp-migration
description: Project-specific shortcut for NS Scrap ERP rehabilitation, database redesign, Supabase Auth/RLS, schema mapping, migration planning, reconciliation, and phase prioritization. Use in this repo when a task affects DB model, migration docs, Supabase environments, master data, auth/permissions, or migration assumptions; read AGENTS.md as the central source of truth.

name: ns-scrap-erp-obsidian
description: Project-specific shortcut for NS Scrap ERP Obsidian notes, MOCs, decision logs, ADR-style notes, migration document links, frontmatter, tags, aliases, and vault navigation. Use in this repo when editing docs/notes or Obsidian-oriented project documentation; read AGENTS.md as the central source of truth.

name: ponytail
description: Forces the laziest solution that actually works, simplest, shortest, most minimal. Channels a senior dev who has seen everything: question whether the task needs to exist at all (YAGNI), reach for the standard library before custom code, native platform features before dependencies, one line before fifty. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra.

name: ponytail-review
description: Code review focused exclusively on over-engineering. Finds what to delete: reinvented standard library, unneeded dependencies, speculative abstractions, dead flexibility. One line per finding: location, what to cut, what replaces it.

name: ponytail-audit
description: Whole-repo audit for over-engineering. Like ponytail-review, but scans the entire codebase instead of a diff: a ranked list of what to delete, simplify, or replace with stdlib/native equivalents.

name: ponytail-debt
description: Harvest every ponytail: comment in the codebase into a debt ledger, so the deliberate shortcuts and deferrals ponytail leaves behind get tracked instead of rotting into later means never.

name: ponytail-help
description: Quick-reference card for all ponytail modes, skills, and commands.
